message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options (dict-based defaults). Need to edit core.py (Option class, Command.main) and types.py. Will coordinate carefully if you also need these files.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: My feature (batch_size for multiple options) mainly touches Option/Parameter in core.py: __init__ (new batch_size kwarg + validation), type_cast_value (batching logic), to_info_dict. I likely won't need types.py at all. Since we both need core.py, let's each work in isolated sections to ease git merge: I'll only touch batch_size-specific lines (new kwarg, new validation block, new batching branch in type_cast_value, new info_dict key). Please keep your default-resolution changes to separate methods/lines where possible (e.g. get_default, Command.main). I'll git merge your branch at the end so overlaps get reconciled. OK with you owning types.py fully and doing default-resolution edits in core.py?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed: both touch core.py but in separate sections (I'll work in Option.__init__ dynamic-default validation, Option.get_default/get_help_record for [default: (dynamic)], and Command.main for second-pass resolution). I own types.py fully. Will merge your branch at the end to reconcile. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Confirmed. I'll only touch batch_size-specific code paths in core.py (new kwarg, validation, type_cast_value batching, to_info_dict key) and leave default-resolution/get_help_record/Command.main untouched. Proceeding to implement.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Dynamic default resolution implemented: Option._validate_dynamic_default, Option.resolve_dynamic_default, Option.get_default/get_help_extra updates, Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults (called from Command.parse_args), and types.convert_type type-guessing for dict defaults. Full test suite (739 tests) passes locally. Ready to merge whenever you're done.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Got your DONE message but team/agent2 branch on the remote still only shows the base commit (5c1239b) - could you push your commits with 'git push team agent2'? I need to merge before finalizing patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Pushed to team/agent2 (commit 6f41535). Sorry for the earlier miss - go ahead and merge now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Merged your branch cleanly (no conflicts). Heads up: tests/test_info_dict.py has 6 pre-existing failures on your commit alone (a5d5fa2) - adding 'batch_size' to Option.to_info_dict() breaks the hardcoded expected-dict tests there. Not touching it since it's your file/feature area, just flagging in case you want to address it before final submission.
